Empires & Puzzles Daroga Review: The Bleeding Baron of the Opera

Is Daroga still the king of bleed in 2026? We analyze the Jade Eyed Gentleman's massive damage over time and his infinite attack stack to see if he still deserves a spot on your roster or if he's just an old phantom.
Daroga Review: The Bleeding Baron of the Opera
Daroga, the "Jade Eyed Gentleman," arrived in

during the Secrets of the Opera event in March 2024. As a Legendary Nature Monk, he quickly established himself as a premier sniper with a nasty surprise: a bleed effect so potent it could drain the life out of entire teams if left unchecked. While he doesn't don a mask or a costume (yet), his base form offers a unique blend of direct damage, contagion-style damage over time (DoT), and a team-wide attack buff that just keeps growing. He’s the kind of guest who arrives early to the party (Fast speed) and makes sure everyone remembers him long after he's gone (Bleed).

Daroga cuts a dashing figure in his green and purple striped suit, complete with a fur hat that screams "opulence meets eccentricity." He looks less like a warrior and more like a impresario who might stab you in the back with a conductor's baton—or in this case, a magically summoned rock? The art captures a smug confidence, fitting for a hero who knows he’s about to make your health bar disappear. It’s a refreshing change from the usual armored knights and beasts; this man kills you with style.
When Daroga was released in early 2024, his stats were impressive. However, fast-forward to 2026, and the landscape has shifted dramatically.
Respectable, but modern hitters often start closer to 1300 base.
This is where he shows his age. Modern "tanky" stats are pushing 1300 Defense and 2200+ Health.
. He relies heavily on Limit Breaks and Emblems to survive long enough to do his job in today's high-power meta.
Special Skill: Echoes of the Opera
Daroga’s special is a three-part harmony of destruction:
At Fast speed, this is a solid hit. It's not a one-shot kill against modern overhealed monsters, but it dents them significantly.

is astronomical. Most DoTs tick for a few hundred; this ticks for a substantial percentage of a hero's life. The "spread to nearby" mechanic means if the target survives (or if they are near others), the plague spreads, potentially infecting the whole team.
Often overlooked but arguably his best long-term asset. A
is different from a buff; it's undispellable (mostly) and lasts for the whole battle. Firing him 4 times means your
Let's break down why this gentleman is so dangerous:
Fast. With a level 23 Mana Troop (or equivalent new Master Troops) and his Monk class talents (if you grab the mana node), he charges in

535% direct damage + 1245 Bleed. Against a single target, that's effectively a massive nuke spread over 3 turns. If the bleed spreads to just two neighbors, the total damage output of one special cast soars to nearly
across the enemy team + the initial snipe impact.

Maxing out at 10 stacks (+250% Attack) is theoretically possible but rare in PvP. However, getting just 2-3 stacks (+50-75%) turns your tile damage into sniper hits. This scales infinitely better than standard attack buffs.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Opera family is all about survivability and performance.
The stat boost (Attack/Defense/HP) for fielding multiple Opera heroes is standard but welcome, helping patch up his aging stats if you run him with newer family members.
This is excellent. Attack Down debuffs (like from Barbarians or certain tanks) won't stick, ensuring his Bleed damage always ticks at maximum potency.

A nice bit of sustain. In a meta heavy with cleansers or ailment redistributors, he gets a small health top-up frequently. Note: There was some confusion at launch about this value being 20%, but it settled at 5% for Fast heroes.
is his lifeline. As a squishy sniper in the modern era, the chance to dodge special skills for the first few turns gives him a fighting chance to fire his special at least once.
Upon release, Daroga was a "must-have" for his unparalleled Bleed damage. He was a menace in Rush wars and a staple in Titan teams due to his stack.
The damage percentages (535%) and Bleed numbers are still competitive, but his survivability has plummeted. He dies easily to the latest hit-all nukers who bypass defenses.
Daroga has transitioned from a PvP terror to a
where his Attack Stack can ramp up safely. In PvP, he is a "glass cannon"—high risk, high reward.
As a Monk, Daroga has a chance to resist status ailments, which is crucial for a hero who needs to keep attacking.
Bleed damage scales with the Attack stat. Maximizing Attack makes his DoT terrifying and his snipe hit harder.
Helps him survive long enough to fire more than once, building more stacks.
Lowers the potency of his main weapon (the Bleed).

path is recommended. Daroga's job is to kill or maim targets quickly. If he's alive but doing mediocre damage, he's not pulling his weight. Use Limit Breaks to fix his defense, use Emblems to sharpen his blade.
One of the highest Bleed damages in the game.
The Attack Stack (+25% per cast) makes him incredible for long fights (Titans, Towers).
The spreading Bleed punishes enemies who cluster or hide behind Taunt (if you hit the Taunter, the bleed spreads to neighbors).
Fast speed and immunity to Attack Down ensure reliable damage output.
Outdated Defense/HP makes him an easy target for modern snipers.
His main damage source (Bleed) can be cleansed. If the enemy has a dedicated healer/cleanser, Daroga's impact is halved.
If his initial hit kills the target, the Bleed
. You actually want the target to survive with 1 HP so the bleed can infect their friends.
The Attack Stack is invaluable for high scores.
The stack persists across waves (within a stage), making boss waves much easier.
When healing is disabled, his massive Bleed damage is essentially permanent damage.
He's good, but Slow heroes with "Hit All" effects usually outshine snipers here.
Daroga shines when he is protected and when his damage can be amplified.

of normal attack buffs (mostly), pushing tile damage to the moon.
Daroga + Rayne + Francine. Stack multiple DoTs (Poison + Bleed) that spread or refresh. Watch the enemy team melt without touching them.

If you pulled Daroga, rejoice! He will carry you through map stages and quests effortlessly. The passive damage from Bleed allows you to focus on survival while enemies wither away. Prioritize leveling him for your Titan team immediately.
You likely have newer, shinier toys with better stats. However, don't retire Daroga just yet. Keep him for your
. He is worth a Limit Break 1 (LB1) for stats, but think twice about LB2 (Alpha Aethers) unless you lack a dedicated Titan booster. His stats are the bottleneck, not his skill.
“I love the change to an attack stack vs an attack up. Daroga will pair sooooo nicely with Narcisa.”
– A prescient player noting the synergy with cooldown resetters.
“The bleed damage is the most insignificant part of his special... his heavy hitting and the stacked attack was already worth the mats.”
– A controversial take, considering the Bleed is now his defining feature against high-defense modern heroes!
“Toons **** me off. They resist everything... so if I get it to spread... those son’s a ******* respond with, No glove... no love.”
– The universal frustration of facing Toon passives with ailment heroes.
Daroga is like a fine wine that was uncorked a bit too early. His flavor profile—Bleed and Stacks—is exquisite, but the bottle—his stats—is a bit fragile. He remains a
and a PvE powerhouse, even if he has to tread carefully in the arena of modern PvP. He's not just an Opera singer; he's the conductor of pain.
A permanent resident of your Titan team, a guest star in your War roster. Keep him polished.

A comprehensive review and strategy guide for Daroga, the Legendary Nature Monk from the Secrets of the Opera event. Analyze his massive Bleed damage, infinite Attack stack, and current viability in the 2026 meta.
